codeReview.summary: Real-time cost tracking implementation for LLM usage is complete and well-structured. The cost_tracking.rs module provides CostRecord for per-call costs using opentelemetry::pricing, CostSummary for aggregation, CostTracker for task/run tracking with BudgetAlert generation at warning (75%) and hard stop (100%) thresholds, and ModelBreakdown for cost breakdown by model including call counts and token totals.

sharedStateObservations.observation: services.yaml file does not exist in mission directory at .factory/services.yaml

library/architecture.md does not exist at .factory/library/architecture.md - this is expected per AGENTS.md which shows the library only contains user-testing.md. The cost tracking implementation correctly uses the existing opentelemetry::pricing module.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ls .factory/library/ shows only user-testing.md; cost_tracking.rs correctly uses use crate::opentelemetry::pricing;

summary: Feature observability-cost-tracking passes scrutiny review. Implementation fully covers all expected behaviors: cost computed per LLM call via pricing::for_model(), aggregated per task via task_summaries HashMap, aggregated per run via run_summary, budget alerts at warning (75%) and hard stop (100%) thresholds, and cost breakdown by model via ModelBreakdown. Code quality is high with proper error handling via thiserror::Error, comprehensive test coverage (13 unit tests + 1 doc test), and all validation passes (cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy). No blocking issues. Integration into swell-core/src/lib.rs is correct with all public exports properly defined.
